title: Two Days From Friday

mood.label: Cautiously hopeful
mood.summary: Day two of the G7 saw the US-Iran war-ending deal move from announced to virtually signed with a Friday ceremony set, markets ran their relief rally a second day and oil eased, while a rebuffed Zelensky, fresh Israeli strikes on Lebanon and Sudan's grinding famine kept the good news at the low edge of good.

style.genre: gospel-soul / chamber-pop — sampled grand piano leading warm gospel-voiced chords over a soft brushed RhythmAce kit, a plucked upright bass and high mallet sparkle, E ionian opening bright but holding one hand back

sampleSources: Strudel default library — sampled grand piano for the gospel chord voicings and the topline, VCSL vibraphone for the high mallet sparkle, RhythmAce drum machine for the soft brushed kit, supporting sine sub-bass and a low triangle pad

lines: The page that ends the war got names on it today, two pens in two cities and a date held up like a lamp,
Friday in Switzerland, a lake and a long table, and a sixty-day clock set ticking on the harder thing they'll stamp;
the markets ran it twice now, green on top of green, the oil eased back like a held breath letting go,
and I caught myself believing — only halfway, only half — the kind of hope you carry where the room is going to know.
Zelensky stood in Évian and offered up his hand, said Europe and the States were in, and Russia turned its back,
and the same wide hour the strikes came down on Lebanon again, and El Fasher keeps its hunger that no rally can walk back;
so the deal is almost paper and the almost is the ache, the good news and the famine running parallel and true,
I'll hold the lift it gives me but I'll hold the other too — two days from Friday, and the ink not dry on you.
Two days from Friday — sing it major, sing it open, E ionian wide as the morning on the lake,
the war agreed but not yet signed, the cure and the crater on the same tide,
I'll let the light come up the wall, I'll let the gospel chord shake — cautiously, the careful joy a careful heart can make;
if the pens behave by Friday I will sing it all the way home, but tonight I keep one hand against the gate.
